message:
feat(fuzz): add fp-io CSV parser fuzz target with round-trip oracle

Adds a new `fuzz_csv_parse` cargo-fuzz target that exercises the fp-io
CSV reader through a parse/write/reparse oracle, matching the pattern
already used by `fuzz_json_io` and `fuzz_fixture_parse`.

crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs
  * `assert_csv_roundtrip(frame)` helper: encodes a successfully parsed
    DataFrame via `write_csv_string`, reparses via `read_csv_str`, and
    returns an `FpIoError::Io` with "csv round-trip drifted after
    parse/write/reparse" if `DataFrame::equals` disagrees. Mirrors the
    existing `assert_json_roundtrip` / `assert_jsonl_roundtrip`.
  * `fuzz_csv_parse_bytes(input: &[u8])` public entrypoint: lossy-
    decodes UTF-8, calls `read_csv_str`, then gates successful parses
    through `assert_csv_roundtrip`. Parser errors on malformed input
    are acceptable; panics, memory-safety issues, or drift after
    round-trip are failures.
  * Exported `fuzz_csv_parse_bytes` from the test-submodule `use`
    list alongside `fuzz_json_io_bytes` and `fuzz_fixture_parse_bytes`.

fuzz/Cargo.toml
  * Registers the new `[[bin]] fuzz_csv_parse` pointing at
    `fuzz_targets/fuzz_csv_parse.rs`, with `test = false`, `doc =
    false`, `bench = false` to match the other targets.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_csv_parse.rs (new)
  * Minimal libfuzzer driver: caps inputs at 64 KiB (`MAX_CSV_BYTES`
    = 64 * 1024) to bound per-iteration cost, then delegates to
    `fp_conformance::fuzz_csv_parse_bytes`.

crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/adversarial/fuzz_corpus/csv_parse/ (new)
  * Seeded corpus checked in so the target starts with coverage from
    iteration one:
      - simple_valid_seed.csv           (27 B)  — minimal well-formed
      - quoted_newline_valid_seed.csv   (52 B)  — embedded newline in
                                                  a quoted field
      - duplicate_headers_invalid_seed.csv (12 B) — header-row
                                                    rejection path

artifacts/phase2c/ADVERSARIAL_FUZZ_CORPUS.md
  * Documents the new target under "Implemented entrypoint": target
    path, seed corpus path, and a one-line description of the
    parse/write/reparse oracle semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -121,6 +121,9 @@ Each fuzz target corresponds to a threat surface. Targets are defined as `fuzz_t
 
 Implemented entrypoint:
 
+- `fuzz_csv_parse` target: `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_csv_parse.rs`
+- seed corpus: `crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/adversarial/fuzz_corpus/csv_parse/`
+- exercises `read_csv_str()` with parse/write/reparse checks via `write_csv_string()`
 - `fuzz_fixture_parse` target: `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_fixture_parse.rs`
 - seed corpus: `crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/adversarial/fuzz_corpus/fuzz_fixture_parse/`
 - `fuzz_json_io` target: `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_json_io.rs`

@@ -25,5 +25,12 @@ test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_csv_parse"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_csv_parse.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false
+
 [workspace]
 members = ["."]
